texts.orig: Separation of magnesium and lithium from brine using a Desal nanofiltration membrane

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Lithium, as the lightest metal in nature, is used in batteries, lubricants, refrigerants, ceramics, medicines, and fusion, which has attracted much attention [1-7]. In recent years, although global economy has suffered, the performance of the lithium industry is still outstanding. The demand for lithium in global market is increasing rapidly with the promotion of new energy, automotive, and electronics industries [8,9]. However, lithium ore resources cannot meet this demand. Therefore, salt lakes and seas with a much larger lithium resource are being considered [10]. Currently, the ion-sieve adsorption method is mainly used to recover lithium from aqueous solutions. However, the Mg 2+ in brine with a high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio is easily hydrolyzed, thus increasing the viscosity and decreasing the pH of the brine. These factors lower the brine diffusion rate of the adsorbent and ion-sieve adsorption capacity. Therefore, a method is urgently needed to decrease the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio, separate magnesium and lithium, and increase the lithium concentration in brine.
texts.text: Lithium, as the lightest metal in nature, is used in batteries, lubricants, refrigerants, ceramics, medicines, and fusion, which has attracted much attention [1-7]. In recent years, although global economy has suffered, the performance of the lithium industry is still outstanding. The demand for lithium in global market is increasing rapidly with the promotion of new energy, automotive, and electronics industries [8,9]. However, lithium ore resources cannot meet this demand. Therefore, salt lakes and seas with a much larger lithium resource are being considered [10]. Currently, the ion-sieve adsorption method is mainly used to recover lithium from aqueous solutions. However, the Mg 2+ in brine with a high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio is easily hydrolyzed, thus increasing the viscosity and decreasing the pH of the brine. These factors lower the brine diffusion rate of the adsorbent and ion-sieve adsorption capacity. Therefore, a method is urgently needed to decrease the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio, separate magnesium and lithium, and increase the lithium concentration in brine.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As a pressure-driven separation process, nanofiltration (NF) technology is based on ultrafiltration and reverse osmosis [11]. This technology has the advantages of a low operating pressure and a high flux and has been widely used in environment and water
texts.text: As a pressure-driven separation process, nanofiltration (NF) technology is based on ultrafiltration and reverse osmosis [11]. This technology has the advantages of a low operating pressure and a high flux and has been widely used in environment and water

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: a b s t r a c t
texts.text: a b s t r a c t

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Owing to the high ratio of Mg 2+ to Li + in most of the salt lake brines in China, it is difficult to extract lithium. Therefore, the separation efficiency of a nanofiltration membrane was investigated in this study. Operating conditions such as operating pressure, inflow water temperature, pH, and Mg 2+ /Li + ratio were investigated. Relationship between the rejection rates of magnesium and lithium was established. Moreover, the extractions of lithium from salt lake brines were also evaluated. The results indicate that the separation of magnesium and lithium was highly dependent on the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio, operating pressure, and pH. When the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was <20, the competitive coefficient was sensitive to the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio. The permeate flux of membrane for the East Taijiner brine was higher than that for the West Taijiner brine.
texts.text: Owing to the high ratio of Mg 2+ to Li + in most of the salt lake brines in China, it is difficult to extract lithium. Therefore, the separation efficiency of a nanofiltration membrane was investigated in this study. Operating conditions such as operating pressure, inflow water temperature, pH, and Mg 2+ /Li + ratio were investigated. Relationship between the rejection rates of magnesium and lithium was established. Moreover, the extractions of lithium from salt lake brines were also evaluated. The results indicate that the separation of magnesium and lithium was highly dependent on the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio, operating pressure, and pH. When the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was <20, the competitive coefficient was sensitive to the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio. The permeate flux of membrane for the East Taijiner brine was higher than that for the West Taijiner brine.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: treatment fields [12]. On account of the special pore-size distribution and preparation technology, NF membranes have a high specific separation performance. These membranes can retain substances with molecular weights between 200 and 1000; moreover, they selectively capture bivalent and multivalent ions. Monovalent ions and small molecules are rejected relatively slowly by these membranes. Because of these characteristics of NF membranes, Somrani et al. [13] studied the separation of lithium from salt lake brines by the NF technology. All the studies were performed using a low flow-rate ratio. This method efficiently separated magnesium and lithium, affording a lithium-rich brine that was converted to lithium carbonate or lithium chloride. Wen et al. [14] analyzed the results of lithium extraction by a DK NF membrane and found that Donnan exclusion, dielectric exclusion, and steric hindrance are the main influencing factors of NF performance. Furthermore, when ionic radius and membrane pore size were close, steric hindrance played a significant role in the separation.
texts.text: treatment fields [12]. On account of the special pore-size distribution and preparation technology, NF membranes have a high specific separation performance. These membranes can retain substances with molecular weights between 200 and 1000; moreover, they selectively capture bivalent and multivalent ions. Monovalent ions and small molecules are rejected relatively slowly by these membranes. Because of these characteristics of NF membranes, Somrani et al. [13] studied the separation of lithium from salt lake brines by the NF technology. All the studies were performed using a low flow-rate ratio. This method efficiently separated magnesium and lithium, affording a lithium-rich brine that was converted to lithium carbonate or lithium chloride. Wen et al. [14] analyzed the results of lithium extraction by a DK NF membrane and found that Donnan exclusion, dielectric exclusion, and steric hindrance are the main influencing factors of NF performance. Furthermore, when ionic radius and membrane pore size were close, steric hindrance played a significant role in the separation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The lithium recovery from brine with a high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio by a Desal (DL) NF membrane have not been studied. In this study, a DL NF membrane was used to study the separation and enrichment of lithium from brine with a high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio systematically. The influencing factors such as the solution concentration, operating pressure, water inlet temperature, and pH of solution were systematically investigated. Moreover, the effects of Na + and Ca 2+ on the separation of magnesium and lithium were evaluated. Finally, the separation of magnesium and lithium was conducted by using simulate East/West Taijiner brine containing rich lithium resources.
texts.text: The lithium recovery from brine with a high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio by a Desal (DL) NF membrane have not been studied. In this study, a DL NF membrane was used to study the separation and enrichment of lithium from brine with a high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio systematically. The influencing factors such as the solution concentration, operating pressure, water inlet temperature, and pH of solution were systematically investigated. Moreover, the effects of Na + and Ca 2+ on the separation of magnesium and lithium were evaluated. Finally, the separation of magnesium and lithium was conducted by using simulate East/West Taijiner brine containing rich lithium resources.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.1. Apparatus and membrane
texts.text: 2.1. Apparatus and membrane

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The schematic diagram of the experimental apparatus is shown in Fig. 1. The feed was first filtered through the filter to remove the particle impurities. Then, the solutions were pumped to the membrane module. The feed flow rate was controlled by returning a part of the solutions into the feed storage barrels. The flow rates of penetrant and concentrated solution were measured using the rotor flow meter. The penetrant and concentrated solutions were fed into the raw liquid tank to stabilize its feed concentrations. A DL-2540 membrane was used as the NF membrane module, and the working parameters are shown in Table 1. The experimental solutions were prepared from LiCl, MgCl2, CaCl2, NaCl (AR), and deionized water (conductivity <0.5 𝛍 s cm -1 ).
texts.text: The schematic diagram of the experimental apparatus is shown in Fig. 1. The feed was first filtered through the filter to remove the particle impurities. Then, the solutions were pumped to the membrane module. The feed flow rate was controlled by returning a part of the solutions into the feed storage barrels. The flow rates of penetrant and concentrated solution were measured using the rotor flow meter. The penetrant and concentrated solutions were fed into the raw liquid tank to stabilize its feed concentrations. A DL-2540 membrane was used as the NF membrane module, and the working parameters are shown in Table 1. The experimental solutions were prepared from LiCl, MgCl2, CaCl2, NaCl (AR), and deionized water (conductivity <0.5 𝛍 s cm -1 ).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.2. Experimental procedure
texts.text: 2.2. Experimental procedure

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The pristine NF membranes were soaked in deionized water for 24 h at room temperature to ensure that the pores of NF membranes were completely wet and stretched. Then, the membranes were washed with deionized water for 2-3 h under low-flow and low-pressure conditions until the preservatives and other chemicals attached to the surface of the membranes were removed. Finally, the entire cycle was repeated for 3 h to preload the NF membranes effectively and to ensure a stable operation in the subsequent experiments.
texts.text: The pristine NF membranes were soaked in deionized water for 24 h at room temperature to ensure that the pores of NF membranes were completely wet and stretched. Then, the membranes were washed with deionized water for 2-3 h under low-flow and low-pressure conditions until the preservatives and other chemicals attached to the surface of the membranes were removed. Finally, the entire cycle was repeated for 3 h to preload the NF membranes effectively and to ensure a stable operation in the subsequent experiments.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The permeate flux of pure water was determined to calculate the permeate coefficient, then the pore sizes of membrane were determined. The operating conditions such as operating pressure, inflow water temperature, pH, and Mg 2+ /Li + ratio were investigated. Relationship between the rejection rates of magnesium and lithium was established. Moreover, the extractions of lithium from salt lake brines were also evaluated
texts.text: The permeate flux of pure water was determined to calculate the permeate coefficient, then the pore sizes of membrane were determined. The operating conditions such as operating pressure, inflow water temperature, pH, and Mg 2+ /Li + ratio were investigated. Relationship between the rejection rates of magnesium and lithium was established. Moreover, the extractions of lithium from salt lake brines were also evaluated

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The total influent flow ( C Li + = 0.152 g L -1 , C Mg 2+ = 9.12 g L -1 , and n Mg 2+ /n Li + = 60) was conducted at a cross-flow rate of 600 L h -1 , which was controlled using the bypass and concentrated-solution exit valves, to weaken the concentration polarization phenomenon. The operating pressure ranged from 1.0 to 2.0 MPa with a step length of 0.2 MPa, and the experiments were carried out at room temperature (18-20 ◦ C).
texts.text: The total influent flow ( C Li + = 0.152 g L -1 , C Mg 2+ = 9.12 g L -1 , and n Mg 2+ /n Li + = 60) was conducted at a cross-flow rate of 600 L h -1 , which was controlled using the bypass and concentrated-solution exit valves, to weaken the concentration polarization phenomenon. The operating pressure ranged from 1.0 to 2.0 MPa with a step length of 0.2 MPa, and the experiments were carried out at room temperature (18-20 ◦ C).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: During the process, the operating pressure (1.0-2.4 MPa) and flow velocity were controlled by the rotation rate of high-pressure pump, reflux regulator and into-the-membrane regulator. The pH (3.0-6.0) was adjusted by HCl and NaOH. The temperature (20-35 ◦ C, error: 0.5-1 ◦ C) was controlled by the heat exchanger of the raw liquid tank. The Mg 2+ /Li + ratio (20-90) was adjusted only by the concentration of Mg 2+ .
texts.text: During the process, the operating pressure (1.0-2.4 MPa) and flow velocity were controlled by the rotation rate of high-pressure pump, reflux regulator and into-the-membrane regulator. The pH (3.0-6.0) was adjusted by HCl and NaOH. The temperature (20-35 ◦ C, error: 0.5-1 ◦ C) was controlled by the heat exchanger of the raw liquid tank. The Mg 2+ /Li + ratio (20-90) was adjusted only by the concentration of Mg 2+ .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the experiments with brines, the flow into the membrane should be under 600 L h -1 in order to reduce the concentration polarization phenomenon due to the complicated components and high ion concentrations of brines. The operating pressure should be high (2.0-3.0 MPa), and the temperature was kept at 20-22 ◦ C.
texts.text: In the experiments with brines, the flow into the membrane should be under 600 L h -1 in order to reduce the concentration polarization phenomenon due to the complicated components and high ion concentrations of brines. The operating pressure should be high (2.0-3.0 MPa), and the temperature was kept at 20-22 ◦ C.

texts.orig: The separation efficiency of a NF membrane can be described by the apparent rejection coefficient R obs, separation factor SF, and membrane flux as follows:
texts.text: The separation efficiency of a NF membrane can be described by the apparent rejection coefficient R obs, separation factor SF, and membrane flux as follows:

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: R obs = C F -C p C F × 100% (1)

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: SF = ( C ( M g 2 + ) /C ( Li + ) ) P ( C ( Mg 2 + ) /C ( Li + ) ) F (2)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: where C P , C F , C (Mg 2+ ) , and C (Li + ) are the concentrations of the penetrant, raw solution, magnesium, and lithium, respectively. T is the time consumed by the volume of 1-L penetrant, and S is the effective membrane area. When SF = 1, magnesium and lithium do not separate; when SF < 1, Li + penetrates the membrane preferentially and becomes more concentrated when SF is lower [15].
texts.text: where C P , C F , C (Mg 2+ ) , and C (Li + ) are the concentrations of the penetrant, raw solution, magnesium, and lithium, respectively. T is the time consumed by the volume of 1-L penetrant, and S is the effective membrane area. When SF = 1, magnesium and lithium do not separate; when SF < 1, Li + penetrates the membrane preferentially and becomes more concentrated when SF is lower [15].

texts.orig: 2.3. Analysis
texts.text: 2.3. Analysis

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The ion concentration was measured using an ARCOS FHS12 Inductively Coupled Plasma Optical Emission Spectrometer (ICPOES) (SPECTRO, Germany). The membrane pore size was characterized by determining the rejection of organics. The operating pressure was 0.5 MPa. The organic concentration was measured as the total organic carbon (TOC) content. Moreover, the pH of the solution was measured using a Metrohm 827 pH meter.
texts.text: The ion concentration was measured using an ARCOS FHS12 Inductively Coupled Plasma Optical Emission Spectrometer (ICPOES) (SPECTRO, Germany). The membrane pore size was characterized by determining the rejection of organics. The operating pressure was 0.5 MPa. The organic concentration was measured as the total organic carbon (TOC) content. Moreover, the pH of the solution was measured using a Metrohm 827 pH meter.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Molecular weight cut-off (MWCO) refers to the molecular weight of a solute (neutral organics) that has a 90% rejection in a dilute solute system [18]. According to the pore model [19], the diameter of organic molecules corresponding to the MWCO of NF membrane may be equal to the effective pore size of the membrane. The Stokes-Einstein equation to estimate the radius of organic molecules can be expressed as follows [20]:
texts.text: Molecular weight cut-off (MWCO) refers to the molecular weight of a solute (neutral organics) that has a 90% rejection in a dilute solute system [18]. According to the pore model [19], the diameter of organic molecules corresponding to the MWCO of NF membrane may be equal to the effective pore size of the membrane. The Stokes-Einstein equation to estimate the radius of organic molecules can be expressed as follows [20]:

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: r s = k T 6 /EM/SYND s (6)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: where r s is the Stokes radius of organic molecules, /SYN is the viscosity, D s is the diffusion coefficient, and k is the Boltzmann constant. Because molecular weight can be easily determined, a quantitative relationship between molecular weight and molecular size can be established, and the radius of organic molecules can also be measured using the molecular weight. The molecular weights and Stokes radii of different organics are shown in Table 4.
texts.text: where r s is the Stokes radius of organic molecules, /SYN is the viscosity, D s is the diffusion coefficient, and k is the Boltzmann constant. Because molecular weight can be easily determined, a quantitative relationship between molecular weight and molecular size can be established, and the radius of organic molecules can also be measured using the molecular weight. The molecular weights and Stokes radii of different organics are shown in Table 4.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2. Effect of operating conditions
texts.text: 3.2. Effect of operating conditions

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2.1. Effect of inflow water temperature and operating pressure
texts.text: 3.2.1. Effect of inflow water temperature and operating pressure

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As shown in Fig. 5(a), the rejection of Li + had a negative value and the rejection of Mg 2+ had a positive value, indicating that Li + penetrated the membrane preferentially and became more concentrated. However, the Mg 2+ was rejected mostly, resulting in the separation of magnesium and lithium. In general, the flux of the sol- vent (water) increased with the pressure. Although the drag effect of ions also increased accordingly, Mg 2+ with a larger hydrated radius was less affected by the pressure when passing through the membrane. Therefore, the concentration of Mg 2+ in the permeation decreased with increasing solvent flux, whereas the retention rate increased as shown in Fig. 5(a). In contrast, the retention rate of Li + decreased with increasing pressure, indicating that Li + with a smaller hydration radius increased the permeate amount. This increased the concentration of the permeation, thus increasing the lithium concentration.
texts.text: As shown in Fig. 5(a), the rejection of Li + had a negative value and the rejection of Mg 2+ had a positive value, indicating that Li + penetrated the membrane preferentially and became more concentrated. However, the Mg 2+ was rejected mostly, resulting in the separation of magnesium and lithium. In general, the flux of the sol- vent (water) increased with the pressure. Although the drag effect of ions also increased accordingly, Mg 2+ with a larger hydrated radius was less affected by the pressure when passing through the membrane. Therefore, the concentration of Mg 2+ in the permeation decreased with increasing solvent flux, whereas the retention rate increased as shown in Fig. 5(a). In contrast, the retention rate of Li + decreased with increasing pressure, indicating that Li + with a smaller hydration radius increased the permeate amount. This increased the concentration of the permeation, thus increasing the lithium concentration.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The temperature error in the experiment was maintained at ± 0.5 ◦ C. According to the osmotic pressure formula: /SOH/EM = RT ∑ Ci , the osmotic pressure increased with temperature, which did not improve the membrane flux. The obtained results are shown in Fig. 5(b). With the increase in temperature, the solution viscosity decreased, and the diffusion coefficient of water increased, thus weakening the concentration polarization phenomenon and improving the flux. Moreover, the change in temperature affected the membrane pore size and ion diffusion coefficient. Further, the ion flow rate also increased accordingly. The ion-retention rate showed a downward trend, particularly in the low-pressure range.
texts.text: The temperature error in the experiment was maintained at ± 0.5 ◦ C. According to the osmotic pressure formula: /SOH/EM = RT ∑ Ci , the osmotic pressure increased with temperature, which did not improve the membrane flux. The obtained results are shown in Fig. 5(b). With the increase in temperature, the solution viscosity decreased, and the diffusion coefficient of water increased, thus weakening the concentration polarization phenomenon and improving the flux. Moreover, the change in temperature affected the membrane pore size and ion diffusion coefficient. Further, the ion flow rate also increased accordingly. The ion-retention rate showed a downward trend, particularly in the low-pressure range.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2.2. Effect of solution pH
texts.text: 3.2.2. Effect of solution pH

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Charging property is one of the most important characteristics of NF membranes. Nowadays, commercially available NF membranes have a certain charge, which varies according to the membrane materials and manufacturing processes. This makes the entrapment mechanism of NF membranes different from the traditional mechanical sieving machine including the electrical interaction between the membrane and organics and that between the membrane and inorganics. Therefore, the pH of a liquid material, to a certain extent, affected the NF membrane properties such as surface charge, hydrophilicity, and aperture.
texts.text: Charging property is one of the most important characteristics of NF membranes. Nowadays, commercially available NF membranes have a certain charge, which varies according to the membrane materials and manufacturing processes. This makes the entrapment mechanism of NF membranes different from the traditional mechanical sieving machine including the electrical interaction between the membrane and organics and that between the membrane and inorganics. Therefore, the pH of a liquid material, to a certain extent, affected the NF membrane properties such as surface charge, hydrophilicity, and aperture.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The pH of the solution (the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was 60) was controlled in the range from 3.0 to 6.0 with HCl. As shown in Fig. 6, the rejection of magnesium increased with pH; however, the rejection of lithium decreased with pH. Therefore, the SF decreased, thus increasing

texts.label: text
texts.orig: the separation of magnesium and lithium. In general, the surface charge of a NF membrane is zero in a certain pH range, known as the isoelectric point [22]. The isoelectric point of Desal-5 series NF membrane is between 4.25 and 4.55 [22].
texts.text: the separation of magnesium and lithium. In general, the surface charge of a NF membrane is zero in a certain pH range, known as the isoelectric point [22]. The isoelectric point of Desal-5 series NF membrane is between 4.25 and 4.55 [22].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As shown by the experimental data, when the pH was 4.5 or 5.6, higher than the isoelectric point, the separation efficiency was almost the same. However, when the pH decreased to 3.9 or less, lower than the isoelectric point, the separation efficiency increased. However, simultaneously, the water flux of the membrane decreased with pH, to a certain degree. This was probably because the decrease in Mg 2+ rejection increased the Mg 2+ concentration in the fresh water side, thus decreasing the osmotic pressure on either side of the membrane. This would reduce the driving force and flux of water [22].
texts.text: As shown by the experimental data, when the pH was 4.5 or 5.6, higher than the isoelectric point, the separation efficiency was almost the same. However, when the pH decreased to 3.9 or less, lower than the isoelectric point, the separation efficiency increased. However, simultaneously, the water flux of the membrane decreased with pH, to a certain degree. This was probably because the decrease in Mg 2+ rejection increased the Mg 2+ concentration in the fresh water side, thus decreasing the osmotic pressure on either side of the membrane. This would reduce the driving force and flux of water [22].

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2.3. Effect of Mg 2+ /Li + ratio
texts.text: 3.2.3. Effect of Mg 2+ /Li + ratio

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was increased by increasing the Mg 2+ concentration (adding MgCl2) to the unchanged Li + concentration ( C (Li + ) = 0.132 g L -1 ). The examined range of Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was between 45 and 90 at room temperature. The influent flow was controlled at 600 L h -1 . Because the NF membrane was driven by pressure, the osmotic pressure would increase with the solute concentration in the solution. Therefore, the membrane separation efficiency of the feed with different concentrations would be very significantly even under the same operating conditions.
texts.text: The Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was increased by increasing the Mg 2+ concentration (adding MgCl2) to the unchanged Li + concentration ( C (Li + ) = 0.132 g L -1 ). The examined range of Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was between 45 and 90 at room temperature. The influent flow was controlled at 600 L h -1 . Because the NF membrane was driven by pressure, the osmotic pressure would increase with the solute concentration in the solution. Therefore, the membrane separation efficiency of the feed with different concentrations would be very significantly even under the same operating conditions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As shown in Fig. 7, the increase in Mg 2+ concentration significantly affected the properties of NF membrane. The rejection of magnesium significantly decreased with the increase in Mg 2+ concentration. In contrast, the rejection of lithium increased. Therefore, the SF increased, which did not help in the separation of magnesium and lithium. The concentration of cations increased with the salt concentration, thus weakening the charge effect between the fixed groups on the membrane surface and charged ions. Secondly, with the increase in the ion concentration near the membrane surface, the driving force of Mg 2+ across the membrane increases. Therefore, more Mg 2+ penetrate the membrane, thus decreasing the rejection of Mg 2+ . The proportion of Li + was relatively smaller. This allows Mg 2+ instead of Li + to penetrate the membrane, thus increasing the rejection of Li + . Simultaneously, an increase in ion concentration strengthened the concentration polarization, which played an important role in decreasing the water flux.
texts.text: As shown in Fig. 7, the increase in Mg 2+ concentration significantly affected the properties of NF membrane. The rejection of magnesium significantly decreased with the increase in Mg 2+ concentration. In contrast, the rejection of lithium increased. Therefore, the SF increased, which did not help in the separation of magnesium and lithium. The concentration of cations increased with the salt concentration, thus weakening the charge effect between the fixed groups on the membrane surface and charged ions. Secondly, with the increase in the ion concentration near the membrane surface, the driving force of Mg 2+ across the membrane increases. Therefore, more Mg 2+ penetrate the membrane, thus decreasing the rejection of Mg 2+ . The proportion of Li + was relatively smaller. This allows Mg 2+ instead of Li + to penetrate the membrane, thus increasing the rejection of Li + . Simultaneously, an increase in ion concentration strengthened the concentration polarization, which played an important role in decreasing the water flux.

texts.orig: 3.3. Competitive relationship between magnesium and lithium
texts.text: 3.3. Competitive relationship between magnesium and lithium

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To prepare MgCl2 solutions with different concentrations, a quantitative amount of LiCl was added into the solution at a constant pressure (0.1 MPa). The influent flux was still maintained at a speed of 600 L h -1 . The experiment was carried out at room temperature without considering the effect of temperature and pH.
texts.text: To prepare MgCl2 solutions with different concentrations, a quantitative amount of LiCl was added into the solution at a constant pressure (0.1 MPa). The influent flux was still maintained at a speed of 600 L h -1 . The experiment was carried out at room temperature without considering the effect of temperature and pH.

texts.orig: The ion rejections of the DL membrane for the simulated East Taijiner brine decreased in the following order: R (Mg 2+ ) > R (Ca 2+ ) > R (K + ) ≈ R (Na + ) > R (Li + ) (Fig. 10).
texts.text: The ion rejections of the DL membrane for the simulated East Taijiner brine decreased in the following order: R (Mg 2+ ) > R (Ca 2+ ) > R (K + ) ≈ R (Na + ) > R (Li + ) (Fig. 10).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In general, large quantities of monovalent ions are present in raw water. The high concentration of Na + (up to 116.475 g L -1 ) affected the permeation of Li + . Therefore, the rejection of Li + increased. However, the rejection of Li + was always maintained below zero, while the rejections of Mg 2+ and Ca 2+ were relatively high within the operating pressure.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.4.2. Process for the West Taijiner brine
texts.text: 3.4.2. Process for the West Taijiner brine

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The mass ratio of Mg 2+ /Li + in the prepared West Taijiner brine was 64, and the ion rejections are shown in Fig. 12. Similar to the East Taijiner brine, the rejection of Mg 2+ was high, whereas those of monovalent ions were low. Therefore, the competitive relationship between Na + and Li + ions was relatively smaller, leading to the lower rejection of Li + . Moreover, the rejection of Ca 2+ was much lower because the concentration of Ca 2+ was lower and the amount passing through the membrane was larger.

Therefore, the competitive relationship between Na + and Li + ions was relatively smaller, leading to the lower rejection of Li + . Moreover, the rejection of Ca 2+ was much lower because the concentration of Ca 2+ was lower and the amount passing through the membrane was larger.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. Conclusions
texts.text: 4. Conclusions

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DL-2540 NF membrane was used to study the separation of magnesium and lithium from brine at a high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio. The operating conditions and the process of salt lake brines on magnesium and lithium separation were investigated. The conclusions are as follows:
texts.text: DL-2540 NF membrane was used to study the separation of magnesium and lithium from brine at a high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio. The operating conditions and the process of salt lake brines on magnesium and lithium separation were investigated. The conclusions are as follows:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The rejection of magnesium and water flux increased with operating pressure, thus benefiting the separation. The SF slightly increased with temperature, which negatively affected the sepa- ration; the water flux increased accordingly. Moreover, a low pH increased the rejection rate of magnesium and decreased the rejection rate of lithium, thus benefiting the separation. A high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio increased the rejection rate of lithium and decreased the rejection rate of magnesium, thus negatively affecting the separation.
texts.text: The rejection of magnesium and water flux increased with operating pressure, thus benefiting the separation. The SF slightly increased with temperature, which negatively affected the sepa- ration; the water flux increased accordingly. Moreover, a low pH increased the rejection rate of magnesium and decreased the rejection rate of lithium, thus benefiting the separation. A high Mg 2+ /Li + ratio increased the rejection rate of lithium and decreased the rejection rate of magnesium, thus negatively affecting the separation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For the competitive relationship between magnesium and lithium, when the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio was low, the competitive coefficient was sensitive to the Mg 2+ /Li + ratio. When the ratio of Mg 2+ /Li + was >20, the competitive coefficient was almost constant. The relationship between the rejection rates of magnesium and lithium followed the equation:

When the ratio of Mg 2+ /Li + was >20, the competitive coefficient was almost constant. The relationship between the rejection rates of magnesium and lithium followed the equation:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of the East Taijiner brine was less than that of the West Taijiner brine, which favored the permeate flux of membrane.
texts.text: The Mg 2+ /Li + ratio of the East Taijiner brine was less than that of the West Taijiner brine, which favored the permeate flux of membrane.
